items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.

items.q: Why are tropical rainforest soils typically infertile?
items.correct: Heavy rainfall leaches nutrients, and most nutrients are stored in living biomass and rapidly recycled — the soil itself holds very little.
items.misconception: Because so much vegetation grows there, the soil must be fertile.
items.random_wrong: Because rainforest soils are made of clay.
items.why: The lush vegetation hides poor soil. The rainforest works as a tightly closed nutrient cycle; once cleared, the system collapses quickly because the soil cannot sustain growth.

items.q: How do humans affect the carbon cycle?
items.correct: By burning fossil fuels (releasing stored geological carbon to the atmosphere) and deforestation (reducing the biosphere store and releasing carbon).
items.misconception: Humans only affect the carbon cycle by breathing out CO2.
items.random_wrong: Humans affect the carbon cycle by eating plants.
items.why: Human respiration is balanced (we eat carbon that plants captured). The disruption is shifting carbon from long-term geological stores into the active atmospheric cycle.

items.q: Where does the Keeling Curve come from?
items.why: It's Hawaii. “Antarctica” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Methane is how many times more potent than CO2?
items.why: It's 28x. “2x” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How much GtC is stored in oceans (thousands)?
items.why: The real figure is 38 thousand GtC. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
